title: Security evaluation playbook — next steps & MAP draft
preview:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Monroe <alice.monroe@blueharbor.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Security evaluation playbook — next steps & MAP draft

Hi Alice,

Thanks again for the time last week — great to hear where BlueHarbor is prioritizing security control evidence for vendor onboarding. Attached is a first-pass mutual action plan (MAP) that aligns our delivery milestones with your security gates. High-level ask: can we target an onboarding close in the 2-week window ending Dec 15 (ideal for budget/Q4)? If that's aggressive on your side, flag the hard gates you need for an enterprise go-live and we’ll adjust.

Key items in this MAP:
- SOC 2 Type II: we can provide our latest report (redacted) and a control narrative.
- SIG/CAIQ: completed questionnaire answers and exportable spreadsheet.
- DPA: our standard DPA (we’re happy to negotiate a few clauses).
- Data retention / residency: options for 30/90/365-day retention; retention controls at dataset and route level.
- Key management: support for Redwood-managed keys or customer-managed keys (KMS/HSM).

Please review the attached MAP-BlueHarbor-v1.xlsx with the following columns filled where possible: owner, target date, acceptance criteria, and deliverable artifact. If you prefer we put the MAP into HubSpot or a shared Google Sheet, we can mirror it there.

I’ve cc’ed Logan (security lead) to propose dates for the artifacts and Stephanie (legal) will join the thread for DPA redlines.

Attachments: [attachment: MAP-BlueHarbor-v1.xlsx (xlsx, 42KB)]

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
evelyn.hart@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

--
Redwood Inference — production-first LLM inference


From: Alice Monroe <alice.monroe@blueharbor.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Bob Reed <bob.reed@blueharbor.com>
Date: Wed, 01 Dec 2026 08:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Security evaluation playbook — next steps & MAP draft

Evelyn — thanks for the MAP and for the clear breakdown. A few constraints from our side before we anchor dates:

1) SOC 2: we require a SOC 2 Type II report covering the period that overlaps our evaluation window. Redacted copies are acceptable, but we need to see the control coverage for encryption, change control, and incident response.
2) SIG/CAIQ: Ideally completed before procurement approval. If you can provide a filled CAIQ spreadsheet, we can do a technical review; partial answers with timelines are OK but please flag the open items.
3) DPA: Our legal team needs an executed DPA before any production data. If you can send a DocuSign link, that speeds things up.
4) Data retention: default for regulated workloads is 90 days of production data, but we can accept 30-day retention for a scoped PII subset. Please specify how retention is applied (per-model, per-customer, route-based) and what triggers deletion.
5) Logging & audit: we need proof of at least 12 months of audit log retention (or an exported sample) and an explanation of log access controls.

Can you confirm owners and target dates for each gate? Our procurement wants a clear list of open items and who is responsible on the vendor side.

Alice Monroe
Head of Vendor Security | BlueHarbor Financial
alice.monroe@blueharbor.com

[external] Note: Bob Reed on my team will help review SIG answers.


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Monroe <alice.monroe@blueharbor.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 01 Dec 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Security evaluation playbook — next steps & MAP draft

Alice — appreciate the specificity. We mapped owners and proposed target dates in the attached MAP and included draft acceptance criteria in red. Summary below: 

- SOC 2 Type II (owner: Logan Wright) — deliverable: SOC2-Type2-Redwood-2026-redacted.pdf. Target: 2026-12-02 (we can accelerate for the above items; report covers required controls). Acceptance: SOC report + control narrative for encryption/change control/IR.

- SIG/CAIQ (owner: Stephanie Nguyen) — deliverable: SIG-CAIQ-Redwood-answers-2026.xlsx. Target: 2026-12-06. Acceptance: completed spreadsheet with references to evidence or mitigation for each control.

- DPA execution (owner: Evelyn Hart / Legal) — deliverable: Redwood-DPA-v1.2.pdf (DocuSign link below). Target: executed by 2026-12-07.

- Data retention & deletion (owner: Platform — Logan) — options included in the MAP: route-based retention rules, retention for PII subsets, and immediate purge API for scoped datasets. Default proposal: 90-day retention for production; we can enable a 30-day purge for scoped PII routes before go-live. Acceptance: configuration screenshot + retention rule export.

- Logging & audit (owner: Security Ops) — retention: 12 months (hot storage for 90 days, warm for remainder). Deliverable: sample redacted log export and RBAC matrix. Target: 2026-12-06.

Attachments: [attachment: SOC2-Type2-Redwood-2026-redacted.pdf (pdf, 1.1MB)], [attachment: SIG-CAIQ-Redwood-answers-2026.xlsx (xlsx, 210KB)], [attachment: Redwood-DPA-v1.2.pdf (pdf, 95KB)], [attachment: redwood-map-acceptance-criteria.md (md, 6KB)]

If these dates work, we’ll update the HubSpot deal with milestones and set the MAP as the single source of truth. If you need an earlier SIG turn-around we can pull extra headcount to finish by 12/04 — call that out and we’ll re-allocate.

Logan Wright
Director, Security Engineering | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0160

--
Redwood — runtime, routing, and retention controls for inference


From: Alice Monroe <alice.monroe@blueharbor.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Bob Reed <bob.reed@blueharbor.com>
Date: Wed, 01 Dec 2026 12:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Security evaluation playbook — next steps & MAP draft

Logan / Evelyn — thanks, that schedule mostly aligns. Two follow-ups before we lock dates: 

• SIG turnaround: can you shorten SIG to 2026-12-06 -> 2026-12-06 is fine, but if you can push to 2026-12-05 that helps our procurement window. If it requires extra effort, please confirm.
• Logs: we need a sample of redacted audit logs showing authentication events and admin actions; 12 months retention is fine.
• Acceptance criteria: please add a short canary checklist in the MAP for pre-prod acceptance: no P1 incidents for 14 days, p95 inference latency <200ms on the evaluation dataset, and successful end-to-end replay on a 10k-token prompt set.
• DPA: please send DocuSign link; our legal will countersign if no unusual redlines.

If you can confirm owners and provide the DocuSign link by EOD today, we can keep the Dec 15 close target.

Alice Monroe
Head of Vendor Security | BlueHarbor Financial


From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Monroe <alice.monroe@blueharbor.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 01 Dec 2026 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Security evaluation playbook — next steps & MAP draft

Thanks all — quick confirmations and links below: 

- SIG/CAIQ: Owner confirmed (Stephanie). We’ll deliver the completed SIG-CAIQ-Redwood-answers-2026.xlsx by 2026-12-05 EOD (we’ll pull a pair of engineers to hit the earlier date).
- SOC 2: Logan will attach the redacted report (attached).
- DPA: DocuSign link here: https://docusign.com/link/ABC123-Redwood-DPA. Expect countersign by BlueHarbor legal within 24–48 hours once they route it.
- Data retention: default 90 days, option for 30-day scoped purge. We’ll include the retention rule export and a sample deletion run (screenshot + audit event) as deliverables.
- Logging: we’ll provide a redacted sample export and RBAC matrix by 2026-12-06.
- Canary acceptance criteria: added to the MAP and also included in redwood-map-acceptance-criteria.md (attached). Short list: 14d no P1, p95 <200ms on eval workload, end-to-end replay success on 10k prompt set.

Attachments included in this thread: [SOC2-Type2-Redwood-2026-redacted.pdf], [SIG-CAIQ-Redwood-answers-2026.xlsx], [Redwood-DPA-v1.2.pdf], [redwood-map-acceptance-criteria.md], [MAP-BlueHarbor-v1.xlsx]

Next steps (for clarity):
1) Alice/BlueHarbor to review SIG spreadsheet + SOC redaction (target: 12/06).
2) BlueHarbor to countersign DPA via DocuSign (target: 12/02).
3) Redwood provides retention export + log sample (target: 12/06).
4) Upon acceptance of artifacts and canary checks, go-live gating decision (target: 12/15).

If anything urgent, ring me at +1 (650) 555-0133. Looking forward to closing this out.

Best,
Stephanie Nguyen
Senior Counsel, Legal & Compliance | Redwood Inference
stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai

--
[DocuSign link pasted above]

title: Activation clearance & certificate exception — proposed conditional PO and holdback
preview:
From: EdgeScale Ops <operations@edgescale.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@edgescale.io
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: Initial vendor docs for PO setup — COI + W9 attached

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the call earlier — attaching our standard COI (ACORD) and W-9 so procurement can start the PO paperwork.

Two quick notes:
- Our COI lists the insured as EdgeScale Inc.; we don’t currently carry an Additional Insured endorsement naming customer entities by default.
- We can obtain an endorsement naming Redwood Inference, but that will take 7–10 business days from our broker.

Attachments: EdgeScale_COI_ACORD.pdf (ACORD 25), EdgeScale_W9.pdf

Please let us know if procurement can issue a conditional PO or if you need any other documents to proceed. We have a milestone kickoff scheduled for Dec 6 and would like to confirm financing.

Thanks,

Samira Khatri
Operations Manager, EdgeScale Inc.
operations@edgescale.io
+1 (415) 555-0192



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: EdgeScale Ops <operations@edgescale.io>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial vendor docs for PO setup — COI + W9 attached

Samira — thanks for the docs and the timeline.

Kimberly / Mina — looping you in to triage the COI gap. At a glance the certificate doesn’t include an Additional Insured endorsement or waiver of subrogation naming Redwood Inference. Can procurement accept a conditional PO with a limited holdback while they secure the endorsement? If procurement/legal need more context from EdgeScale, please outline the specific endorsement language we require and I’ll escalate.

Action items:
- Kimberly: please advise on conditional PO mechanics and suggested holdback percentage.
- Mina: confirm whether security needs any compensating controls while endorsement is outstanding (e.g., scoped network segmentation, limited data access, or specific SSO constraints).

We’re under a tight milestone schedule so any temporary activation plan would be helpful.

Cheers,
Ben
Sr. Vendor Ops (Sales) 
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 08:42:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Initial vendor docs for PO setup — COI + W9 attached

Thanks Ben — I reviewed the COI. Summary:

- Missing items: Additional Insured endorsement naming "Redwood Inference, its officers, directors, employees and agents"; waiver of subrogation in favor of Redwood; endorsement dates that align with the PO term.
- Exposures: vendor carries primary GL and Excess, but the policy limits shown are slightly below our standard rolling threshold for strategic vendors.

Procurement options (recommendation follows):
1) Conditional PO (preferred): issue PO for the first milestone only (up to $15,000) with a 10% holdback retained until we receive the executed Additional Insured endorsement and an updated COI showing the endorsement. Include clause: "PO conditional on receipt of Additional Insured endorsement within 15 business days; failure to comply may result in holdback or suspension."
2) Full PO with escrow: not preferred for speed.
3) Decline/Delay: for legal-only acceptance if endorsement not possible — unlikely given vendor willingness.

If we take (1), procurement needs:
- Signed SOW or milestone statement for the initial deliverable
- EdgeScale to confirm broker ETA in writing (prefer firm date)
- Billing contact and ACH details (we have W-9)

Mina: please advise whether Security will accept scoped mitigations (VPC isolation + admin role restrictions + limited dataset) for the initial milestone while endorsement is processing. Logan: if we proceed, please prepare a PO draft and DocuSign routing for approval.

-kim
Kimberly Park
Head of Procurement
Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.com



From: EdgeScale Ops <operations@edgescale.io>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 14:27:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Initial vendor docs for PO setup — COI + W9 attached

Hi Kimberly, Ben —

Thanks for the clear options. We can provide the Additional Insured endorsement and a waiver of subrogation. Broker confirms turnaround of 6 business days if we submit today. I have attached a draft endorsement form from our broker for your legal review (proposed-endorsement-draft.pdf).

We are happy to accept the conditional PO with 10% holdback for the initial milestone. Proposed milestone invoices:
- Milestone 1 (onboarding & adapter). Estimated invoiced value: $15,000.
- Milestone 2 (production cutover). Estimated invoiced value: $40,000.

We will:
- Submit the endorsement request to broker by COB today (Nov 25).
- Provide a firm date for issuance by Nov 30.
- Provide signed SOW and billing contact by EOD Nov 26.

Let us know if you need any additional language in the endorsement; otherwise we will instruct the broker to name: "Redwood Inference, its subsidiaries and affiliates" as Additional Insured and include waiver of subrogation.

Best,
Samira Khatri
Operations Manager, EdgeScale Inc.

Attachments: proposed-endorsement-draft.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, EdgeScale Ops <operations@edgescale.io>
Date: Sat, 28 Nov 2026 14:42:00 -0800
Subject: Activation clearance plan + next steps (conditional PO)

Team / Samira — good progress. Summary and proposed immediate plan so we can move to execution: 

1) Authorization: Procurement will issue a conditional PO (PO# REDFW-PO-8891) for Milestone 1 (onboarding & adapter) up to $15,000 with a 10% holdback ($1,500) retained until we receive the broker-issued Additional Insured endorsement and updated COI.

2) Timeline: EdgeScale to confirm endorsement issuance date by Nov 30. Endorsement must be received by Dec 10 (we’ll accept earlier). If endorsement is not delivered by Dec 10, procurement reserves the right to pause further milestone invoices.

3) Security mitigations (temporary): Mina confirmed acceptance of scoped mitigations for the onboarding milestone only — VPC peering with restricted subnets, role-based admin access, no production dataset access. These must be documented in the SOW and validated by Security prior to any work.

4) Docs to collect before routing PO for signature:
- Signed SOW / milestone statement (EdgeScale to send by Nov 26)
- Updated W-9 with billing contact (received)
- Broker confirmation email re: endorsement ETA (EdgeScale sent commitment)
- Final invoice for Milestone 1 at time of billing

5) Routing: Logan — please prepare the PO draft, include conditional endorsement clause, and route via DocuSign to Samira for acceptance. Proposed DocuSign link appended in metadata.

Samira — please confirm you will instruct broker today and share their confirmation email once submitted. Once procurement receives the endorsement we’ll release the holdback and proceed to Milestone 2 planning.

Thanks — moving this to execution.

Ben Carter
Sr. Vendor Ops (Sales)
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

Attachments referenced: EdgeScale_COI_ACORD.pdf, EdgeScale_W9.pdf, proposed-endorsement-draft.pdf

title: Small-batch latency: request for ablation/rollout notes and demo slot
preview:
From: Caroline Nguyen <c.nguyen@smallcard.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 15:24:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SDK question — p99 spikes with small batch sizes

Hi Jonas,

Quick note from our QA team: when we set the SDK batch window to very small values (max_inflight=2 and per_request_batch_max_tokens=64) our p99 for single-turn generation jumps from ~320ms to ~1.8s during peak traffic. We attached the latency trace and the CSV of sampling windows. This started after yesterday's client-side SDK bump (v1.8.3).

Any chance this is a runtime-side behavior? We are in the middle of a pilot and need to understand whether a config change or a platform fix is required before we expand to production.

Thanks,
Caroline
Senior SRE, Smallcard Inc.
Attachment: smallcard_latency_trace.png, smallcard_batch_metrics.csv


From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
To: runtime@redwood.com, platform@redwood.com
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Caroline Nguyen <c.nguyen@smallcard.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:18:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: SDK question — p99 spikes with small batch sizes

Forwarding Caroline's note — customer is seeing p99 spikes when they intentionally force very small batch windows. Notably the workload is single-turn short prompts (30–80 tokens).

Ask: can runtime team confirm whether (a) small in-flight batch sizes can hit a slow-path kernel that increases wakeup overhead, or (b) our adaptive batching/cooling heuristics are delaying dispatch in these configs? If this is config-sensitive, can we propose a short-term mitigation we can ask the customer to toggle on their Dedicated pool?

Also asking product: would a temporary flag (disable_smallbatch_slowpath=true) be reasonable as an emergency rollback? If so, what are the rollout and safety checks?

Jonas Weber
Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Caroline Nguyen <c.nguyen@smallcard.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 10:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: SDK question — p99 spikes with small batch sizes

Thanks for the ping. Quick context from field: several customers intentionally drive small concurrency for latency predictability; we've seen a couple of cases where the runtime's batching heuristics oscillate and add ~1s tail latency.

What I need from runtime: a) confirmation of whether a kernel/wakeup path exists that penalizes very-small batches, b) a short checklist of config toggles we can suggest (per-pool flags), and c) an ablation experiment plan (control vs disable heuristic) we can run with Smallcard next Tues/Wed during their low-traffic window.

I can coordinate the customer schedule and run the demo; would like the runtime team to bring expected metrics to track (p50/p95/p99, CPU/GPU utilization, dispatch delay histogram).

Tessa Morgan
Sales Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 07:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: SDK question — p99 spikes with small batch sizes

Hi team — short summary from runtime investigation: there is an observed dispatch slow-path when batch sizes are below our adaptive window threshold AND when KV cache priming is triggered frequently. The costs appear to be dominated by small-kernel dispatch and async wake cycles.

Temporary mitigations we can roll into Dedicated pools (per-customer flag):
- disable_adaptive_cooling = true (forces immediate dispatch when inflight < threshold)
- prefer_wide_kernel_for_small_batches = true (selects a slightly heavier kernel that has lower per-call overhead)

Caveats: prefer_wide_kernel increases average GPU memory pressure ~3–6% and will slightly raise cost per token for high-throughput customers. We can gate the flag to the customer's pool and revert after the ablation.

Proposed ablation plan (we can run this during the customer's low-traffic slot):
1) Baseline: current flags (collect p50/p95/p99, dispatch delay histogram, gpu_util, kernel selection logs) — 30m
2) Toggle disable_adaptive_cooling = true only — 30m
3) Toggle prefer_wide_kernel_for_small_batches = true only — 30m
4) Both flags on — 30m
5) Rollback and compare metrics, then agree next steps

I'll instrument an ephemeral route and provide a small trace collector (log links) and a script the customer can run to push a controlled workload. Can do the run Tues 24th 09:00–11:00 PST if that works for them.

Attaching: internal runbook stub and the quick-utils script (will place in the drive link).

Marissa Cole
Runtime Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachment: runbook_smallbatch_ablation.md (placed in related Drive folder)


From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: Caroline Nguyen <c.nguyen@smallcard.com>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: SDK question — p99 spikes with small batch sizes

Thanks everyone — confirmation to proceed. Caroline, confirmed Smallcard is available Tue 24 Nov 09:00–10:30 PST for the ablation. Agenda: 5m intro, 60m runs (four 30m buckets with overlap tolerance), 25m debrief.

Action items: 
- Marissa: provision ephemeral route and upload trace collector + script (drive link in related_links) — by Mon EOD.
- Jonas/Tessa: send pre-run checklist to Caroline and get any experiment constraints (rate limits, dataset, expected traffic shape).
- Logan (me): I'll capture the rollback play and propose the temporary flag gating in the console; want the exact flag names and preview of UI modal for customer opt-in by Mon so we can ship a one-click toggle in the console for the Dedicated pool.

I'll follow up with a brief exec note post-run.

Logan Wright
Product Manager, Redwood Inference

title: Remote pay harmonization: proposed EU / APAC adjustments for incoming offers
preview:
From: Emma Chan <emma@talentbridge.co>
To: jasmine_liu@redwood.ai
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Comp bands + offer guidance for remote EU/APAC candidate — need decision

Hi Jasmine,

Quick context: we have an excellent candidate (R. Nakamura) based in Tokyo with strong infra experience. The hiring manager wants to move fast; they prefer a blended offer that aligns to Redwood's US L5 band but takes into account APAC cost-of-living and local market pay practices (base/bonus mix).

Can you confirm the right approach for:
- Base adjustment vs US-equivalent banding (do we target 85–95% of the US median?)
- Whether we should use a COLA stipend vs permanent salary differential for Tokyo
- Any approvals needed for above-band offers or signing bonus > $20k

I can share candidate comp expectations once you confirm the guardrails. Attaching the candidate CV in a follow-up.

Best,
Emma Chan
Talent Partner, TalentBridge
emma@talentbridge.co
+1 (415) 555-0198


From: jasmine_liu@redwood.ai
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: soﬁa.rojas@talentsearch.global, emma@talentbridge.co
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:28:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Comp bands + offer guidance for remote EU/APAC candidate — need decision

Forwarding Emma's note — hiring manager is asking for a fast turn. Logan / Marcus: can you confirm the policy posture for Tokyo hires? Two specific asks: 1) should comp be structured as a % of US median or a localized band? 2) is a one-time COLA stipend permissible instead of permanent uplift?

Context: Candidate would slot at L5 in US, manager prefers to keep level but expects salary compression if we localize. We want to avoid overpaying vs internal peers but remain competitive.

Thanks — Jasmine
Jasmine Liu
Senior Talent Partner | Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: jasmine_liu@redwood.ai, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Remote pay harmonization: proposed EU / APAC adjustments for incoming offers

Hi — I went through the banding models and ran a quick simulation for Tokyo (attached: comp-localization-v3.xlsx). Key recommendations: 

- Target: 90% of US L5 median as the base salary range for Tokyo, with a 5–8% discretionary signing allowance to bridge market expectations.
- Structure: prefer permanent salary uplift vs one-time COLA stipend for senior ICs — it aligns better with long-term retention and benefits calculations. Stipends introduce complexity for taxes and benefits eligibility in APAC.
- Exceptions: approve one-off higher signing bonuses (up to $30k) for hard-to-fill infra roles, requires GM sign-off. Anything above $30k needs compensation committee approval.
- Internal parity: use total cash (base + target bonus + signing allowance) to compare to existing remote L5s in EU/US. If total cash would exceed top of band by >12%, escalate for review.

I've attached a model with three scenarios (conservative / midpoint / aggressive). For R. Nakamura specifically, if we keep level L5 and apply 90% base + $20k sign we land competitive but inside our escalation threshold.

Marcus Lin
Director, Compensation & Benefits
Redwood Inference
Attachment: comp-localization-v3.xlsx


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, jasmine_liu@redwood.ai
Date: Fri, 13 Nov 2026 14:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Remote pay harmonization: proposed EU / APAC adjustments for incoming offers

Thanks Marcus — this seems reasonable. A few notes from the hiring manager side: 1) timeline is tight; they'd like an offer on candidate by end of next week, 2) manager prefers the signing allowance rather than increasing base if that's operationally simpler for payroll.

Operational ask: if we go with a one-time $20k signing allowance, confirm that we can process it without triggering additional benefits or benefits re-pricing in Japan. If that creates complexity, proceed with the 90% base + $15k sign.

Approve the approach in principle; Marcus please mark the scenario we should use for the offer draft.

Logan Wright
VP, Engineering
Redwood Inference


From: jasmine_liu@redwood.ai
To: Emma Chan <emma@talentbridge.co>, Sofia Rojas <sofia.rojas@talentsearch.global>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 16:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Final: Offer posture for R. Nakamura — Tokyo (L5)

Emma / Sofia — thanks for the patience. Final decision for the R. Nakamura offer:

- Level: L5 (same as US band)
- Base: 90% of US L5 median (local currency equivalent)
- Signing: one-time signing allowance of $20,000 (USD) payable after start date payroll cycle
- Bonus: standard Redwood target bonus (pro-rated for start date)
- Approvals: VP approval documented (Logan); Compensation exceptions threshold not exceeded (under $30k).

Next steps for you:
1) Draft the offer packet using the attached candidate-offer-draft-RNakamura.pdf as the template and reflect the pay in JPY.
2) Confirm tax withholding guidance for the signing allowance with payroll — Marcus will coordinate with payroll ops and send final guidance by EOD tomorrow.
3) Candidate sign-off target: close by 2026-11-20.

Onward — Jasmine
Jasmine Liu
Senior Talent Partner | Redwood Inference
Attachments: candidate-offer-draft-RNakamura.pdf

title: Private VPC: Tenant Interface Index & Control/Data Plane Annotations
preview:
From: Elliot Price <elliot@redwood.com>
To: Carlos Reyes <carlos@nimbusedge.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: Private VPC: Tenant Interface Index & Control/Data Plane Annotations

Carlos — attaching a first-pass "tenant interface index" and an annotated control/data-plane diagram for the on-prem VPC handoff. Goal is to clarify responsibilities across the attachment boundary so your infra and our ops teams have an unambiguous index for staging -> prod cutover.

Key asks from our side:
- Confirm which side will own SNAT/eDNA for tenant outbound (we've tentatively put this on your edge routers in the spreadsheet).
- Verify whether you can accept MTU 9000 on the tenant-facing VLAN or if we should constrain to 1500 for now.
- Point us to any internal runbook or change freeze windows we should schedule against.

Attachments: tenant-interface-index.xlsx (tabbed: interfaces, ingress-egress-responsibility, notes) and control-data-annotated-diagram.pdf (control-plane hooks, cert rotation/propagation callouts).

I'll coordinate a short 30m sync after you've had a look; Karthik/Mina are listed to capture the technical mapping and operational ownership.

Thanks,
Elliot
Elliot Price
Senior Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
elliot@redwood.com
Redwood Inference



From: Carlos Reyes <carlos@nimbusedge.com>
To: Elliot Price <elliot@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-02T10:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Private VPC: Tenant Interface Index & Control/Data Plane Annotations

Thanks Elliot — received both attachments and initial read looks good. A couple of clarifications from our networking team:

- SNAT/egress: We prefer to own SNAT at the tenant edge (egress gateway) to preserve source IP for internal telemetry. If Redwood needs stable source IPs for callbacks, we can reserve a small set of public IPs and NAT there — would that work?
- MTU: We run mixed hardware at the edge. MTU 9000 is supported for colocated racks but not across our peering fabrics; if you can keep path MTU at 1500 for control-plane probes and the first cut of data-plane traffic, we can pilot jumbo frames in the colo pockets later.
- Cert propagation: we need explicit TTLs for the control-plane cert exchange window — ops wants a 48-hour window documented so cert rotations don't overlap.

I attached our short notes (nimbusedge-egress-notes.docx) with a tentative public IP block and constraints. Two follow-ups: can you confirm which control-plane endpoints Redwood will present for readiness probes (hostnames + expected ports)? Also looping in our networking lead (we'll add them to the meeting invite if needed).

Carlos Reyes
Head of Partnerships & Network Operations
NimbusEdge
carlos@nimbusedge.com



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Carlos Reyes <carlos@nimbusedge.com>, Elliot Price <elliot@redwood.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-03T14:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Private VPC: Tenant Interface Index & Control/Data Plane Annotations

Carlos — thanks for the quick read and the egress notes. Some technical replies below (pulling from the tenant-interface-index.xlsx).

1) SNAT / Source preservation
- We can support either approach. If NimbusEdge owns SNAT at the edge, Redwood will rely on client-provided X-Forwarded-For for application-layer telemetry. For control-plane callbacks (webhooks, KMS attestation calls) we need a stable egress IP range. Proposed compromise:
  - Reserve 3 static IPs on your egress gateway for control-plane callbacks (listed in your attachment).
  - All other tenant data egress can be owner-by-tenant-edge, no SNAT on our side.

2) MTU handling
- We'll set control-plane probes at 1500. For data-plane we will mark the interface with MTU=1500 in the initial config and expose a readiness flag that allows you to flip to 9000 once colo pockets confirm. Our runtime will handle fragmentation and will prefer smaller segments when the path MTU is unknown.

3) Control-plane endpoints
- Readiness/probe endpoints (for your firewall allow-lists):
  - control-1.redwood-inference.com:443 (TLS)
  - control-2.redwood-inference.com:443 (TLS)
  - metrics-push.redwood-inference.com:9200 (TLS) -- only from the reserved IPs

4) Cert propagation window
- 48-hour overlap is fine. We'll publish a cert-rotation schedule and a simple rollback playbook.

Action items I've taken: updated the tenant-interface-index.xlsx (sheet: ingress-egress-responsibility) with the reserved control-plane IPs and MTU notes. Attaching a quick snippet of the mapping we plan to push to the orchestration templates:

# sample snippet (conceptual)
- interface: tenant-vlan-200
  mtu: 1500
  ingress_handled_by: nimbusedge
  egress_snat: nimbusedge
  control_probe_ips: [control-1.redwood-inference.com, control-2.redwood-inference.com]

I'll sync with Mina on the rollout window and with our networking team to add the specific ACLs.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Principal Network Engineer, Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com



From: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
To: Carlos Reyes <carlos@nimbusedge.com>, Elliot Price <elliot@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-05T16:42:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Private VPC: Tenant Interface Index & Control/Data Plane Annotations — consolidated readout & next steps

Carlos / Team — thanks for the notes and the IP block. Consolidated readout below, plus owners and timelines. I've also attached tenant-interface-index-v2.xlsx which includes the reserved IPs and a simple turn-up checklist.

Summary decisions (agreed):
- NimbusEdge will own tenant egress SNAT for data-plane. Redwood will consume X-Forwarded-For for telemetry and reserve 3 public IPs for control-plane callbacks. (Owner: NimbusEdge networking; ETA: 2026-11-20 for IP reservation)
- Initial MTU for both control and data plane will be 1500. Jumbo frames can be enabled later inside colo pockets per pocket readiness flag. (Owner: Redwood Ops + NimbusEdge colo ops; ETA: pilot by 2026-12-01)
- Cert rotations: 48-hour overlapping window, Redwood will publish rotation schedule and rollback playbook to the Drive folder. (Owner: Redwood Security; ETA: schedule by 2026-11-08)

Rollout / cutover plan (brief):
1) Pre-cutover validation (config pushed to staging tenant slice) — Redwood to run control-probe smoke tests against reserved IPs. (Owner: Karthik / Redwood NetOps)
2) Tenant traffic shadowing window (48h) — mirror traffic to the new attachment and validate telemetry. (Owner: Logan / Redwood SRE)
3) Production flip (scheduled change window) — coordinate with NimbusEdge change freeze window and confirm ACLs. (Owner: Elliot + NimbusEdge network ops)

Open items for next sync:
- Carlos: confirm exact WAN ACLs and the contact for colo pocket readiness confirmation.
- NimbusEdge: share the change freeze blackout periods for November.
- Redwood: publish the cert rotation doc to the Drive link below.

Drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1zX_example_designs
Jira: NETOPS-4823 (follow the checklist in the new spreadsheet)

If there are no objections, I'll schedule the 30m cutover planning call next week (suggested: 2026-11-10 10:00–10:30 PT).

Thanks — Mina
Mina Haddad
Engineering Manager, Production Delivery, Redwood Inference
mina@redwood.com

--

- Initial MTU for both control and data plane will be 1500. Jumbo frames can be enabled later inside colo pockets per pocket readiness flag. (Owner: Redwood Ops + NimbusEdge colo ops; ETA: pilot by 2026-12-01)
- Cert rotations: 48-hour overlapping window, Redwood will publish rotation schedule and rollback playbook to the Drive folder. (Owner: Redwood Security; ETA: schedule by 2026-11-08)

Rollout / cutover plan (brief):
1) Pre-cutover validation (config pushed to staging tenant slice) — Redwood to run control-probe smoke tests against reserved IPs. (Owner: Karthik / Redwood NetOps)
2) Tenant traffic shadowing window (48h) — mirror traffic to the new attachment and validate telemetry. (Owner: Logan / Redwood SRE)
3) Production flip (scheduled change window) — coordinate with NimbusEdge change freeze window and confirm ACLs. (Owner: Elliot + NimbusEdge network ops)

Open items for next sync:
- Carlos: confirm exact WAN ACLs and the contact for colo pocket readiness confirmation.
- NimbusEdge: share the change freeze blackout periods for November.
- Redwood: publish the cert rotation doc to the Drive link below.

Drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1zX_example_designs
Jira: NETOPS-4823 (follow the checklist in the new spreadsheet)

If there are no objections, I'll schedule the 30m cutover planning call next week (suggested: 2026-11-10 10:00–10:30 PT).

Thanks — Mina
Mina Haddad
Engineering Manager, Production Delivery, Redwood Inference
mina@redwood.com

--
On Mon, Nov 3, 2026 at 2:05 PM Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> Carlos — thanks for the quick read and the egress notes. Some technical replies below (pulling from the tenant-interface-index.xlsx).
>
> 1) SNAT / Source preservation
> - We can support either approach. If NimbusEdge owns SNAT at the edge, Redwood will rely on client-provided X-Forwarded-For for application-layer telemetry. For control-plane callbacks (webhooks, KMS attestation calls) we need a stable egress IP range. Proposed compromise:
>   - Reserve 3 static IPs on your egress gateway for control-plane callbacks (listed in your attachment).
>   - All other tenant data egress can be owner-by-tenant-edge, no SNAT on our side.
>
> 2) MTU handling
> - We'll set control-plane probes at 1500. For data-plane we will mark the interface with MTU=1500 in the initial config and expose a readiness flag that allows you to flip to 9000 once colo pockets confirm. Our runtime will handle fragmentation and will prefer smaller segments when the path MTU is unknown.
>
> 3) Control-plane endpoints
> - Readiness/probe endpoints (for your firewall allow-lists):
>   - control-1.redwood-inference.com:443 (TLS)
>   - control-2.redwood-inference.com:443 (TLS)
>   - metrics-push.redwood-inference.com:9200 (TLS) -- only from the reserved IPs
>
> 4) Cert propagation window
> - 48-hour overlap is fine. We'll publish a cert-rotation schedule and a simple rollback playbook.
>
> -Karthik

title: BrightLeaf walkthrough + handoff rhythm (DST offset impact)
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Sylvie Moreau <sylvie.moreau@brightleaf.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-30T15:12:00-04:00
Subject: Scheduling: BrightLeaf product walkthrough & follow-up handoff

Karthik / Logan / Sylvie —

Thanks for making time today. Quick note on scheduling logistics: EU just fell back to CET on Oct 25 while the US remains on EDT until Nov 1. That means the current difference is 5 hours (e.g., 10:30 ET = 15:30 CET) but will jump to 6 hours after Nov 1. We’ve already had one confused invite when the customer used CET labels and we interpreted as CEST.

Sylvie, per our discovery notes you said the preferred window is late morning CET for your team (09:00–11:30 CET). We want to make sure the internal handoff (US SDR -> EU AE) and the 30–45m follow-up are covered without waking APAC too late.

Two asks for internal alignment:
- Karthik / Logan: can we land on a repeatable rotation (who owns the follow-up and which local slot) that survives the DST transition on Nov 1?
- Evelyn: any constraints on AE capacity for late EU slots + same-day follow-ups?

I sketched a quick matrix (attached) with a 2-week rotation + fallback rules. If we agree I’ll update the calendar template to show both ET/CET and an auto-fallback note for post-Nov 1.

Attachments: [Attachment: handoff_rotations_v2.pdf (PDF, 78KB)]

Thanks —
Aisha Rahman
SDR, Redwood Inference
aisha.rahman@redwood.com

---
On Oct 30, 2026, at 14:03, Karthik Iyer wrote:
> (planning note) we should not auto-convert times in the invite body; include both local examples and UTC.


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sylvie Moreau <sylvie.moreau@brightleaf.com>
Date: 2026-10-30T16:05:00-04:00
Subject: RE: Scheduling: BrightLeaf product walkthrough & follow-up handoff

Thanks Aisha — useful flag. Quick proposal to make the handoff predictable across the DST mismatch:

Rotation proposal (week-by-week):
1) Weeks with the main customer call scheduled between Mon–Thu morning CET:
   - SDR (US) books primary call in customer local time; AE (EU) takes ownership of same-day follow-up if the call ends before 12:00 CET.
   - If call ends after 12:00 CET, AE owns a next-business-day follow-up to avoid late-night APAC exposure.
2) Weeks with customer AM ET-leaning asks (customer prefers US morning):
   - SDR owns the call and the immediate follow-up; AE scheduled in for a 24–48h tactical deep-dive.
3) Fallback from Nov 1 (when US falls back):
   - Include UTC time in the invite and add a single line in the description: "Note: US/EU DST mismatch Nov 1–7 may shift local times by one hour; confirm the local time in the event RSVP."
4) APAC coverage: asynchronous notes + 10–15m recording snippet; if APAC requires live, AE to coordinate an alternate 30m slot within 48h.

I attached the rotation matrix and a mutual action plan draft. Ask: Logan — can you confirm you’re ok taking same-day follow-ups for calls that end before 12:00 CET? If not, suggest an alternate cutoff.

Attachments: [Attachment: handoff_rotations_v2.pdf (PDF, 78KB)], [Attachment: BrightLeaf_Mutual_Action_Plan.xlsx (XLSX, 45KB)]

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0142


From: Sylvie Moreau <sylvie.moreau@brightleaf.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T08:22:00+01:00
Subject: Re: Scheduling: BrightLeaf product walkthrough & follow-up handoff

Thanks — that proposal makes sense. For BrightLeaf, we prefer 09:30 CET for the walkthrough (team availability).

A couple quick preferences:
- 09:30 CET (local) for the primary walkthrough; 30–45m length.
- If there’s a same-day follow-up, 16:30–17:30 CET works best (we have engineers available then).

We can confirm recurring slots once you confirm the rotation. Also — please include a brief recording/call notes for any APAC colleagues who can’t join live.

Looking forward —
Sylvie Moreau
Head of Partnerships, BrightLeaf
sylvie.moreau@brightleaf.com
+33 6 12 34 56 78

---
On Fri, Oct 30, 2026 at 04:05 PM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:
> (see rotation matrix attached)

Date: 2026-11-02T09:03:00+01:00
Subject: Re: Scheduling: BrightLeaf product walkthrough & follow-up handoff

Sylvie — 09:30 CET works for me for the walkthrough. I’ll take the same-day follow-up window at 16:30 CET next Tuesday (Nov 10) if that’s still open on your side — otherwise I’ll coordinate a 24–48h deep-dive.

I’ll update the calendar invite to include both ET/CET and UTC examples and add the recording note for APAC. Karthik / Aisha — I’ll own the follow-up on the rotation week of Nov 8–14.

title: Plain‑language DPA & subprocessor wording for hosted API console
preview:
From: Maria Gomez <maria.gomez@globexanalytics.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@globexanalytics.com
Date: Tue, 27 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick: public copy for DPA/subprocessor list in our procurement packet

Hi Karthik,

We're going through procurement for the PoC and the legal team asked for a 1–2 sentence plain‑language blurb we can paste in our vendor security sheet and another short line for our internal console screenshot (customer-facing content). They want: what data is transferred, who the subprocessors are (or where we can find a current list), and where to find the DPA.

High level ask: can you send two short options (one very concise, one a slightly expanded sentence) we can use in the procurement packet + a UI blurb for the console (approx 1 line + tooltip text)? Also: what cadence do you update the subprocessor list, and can you provide a CSV export for our procurement tracker?

Appreciate a quick turnaround — procurement would like something by EOD tomorrow if possible.

Thanks,
Maria
Procurement Lead
Globex Analytics



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Maria Gomez <maria.gomez@globexanalytics.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Oct 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick: public copy for DPA/subprocessor list in our procurement packet

Hi Maria — thanks for the note and timeline. Looping in Kimberly (Legal) for the DPA phrasing and Laura (Product) for console UI copy. Logan is on our compliance team and will prepare the subprocessor CSV.

Quick answers: we publish the subprocessor list as a live CSV and update it weekly; we also snapshot it when there are material changes. I'll attach the latest DPA draft and the current export for procurement to use as a starting point.

Action items I'll take:
- Kimberly and Laura: can you propose two public‑facing sentence options and a compact tooltip text for the console? One concise and one slightly expanded so procurement has a choice.
- Logan: please attach the CSV and confirm update cadence.
- Maria: procurement deadline is noted; we'll aim for EOD tomorrow.

Attaching: redwood_dpa_draft_v2.pdf (working draft) and subprocessor_map_q42026.csv (latest export).

Thanks all — Karthik
Head of Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: redwood_dpa_draft_v2.pdf (PDF, 132KB)]
[Attachment: subprocessor_map_q42026.csv (CSV, 24KB)]



From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Maria Gomez <maria.gomez@globexanalytics.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Oct 2026 11:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick: public copy for DPA/subprocessor list in our procurement packet

Hi — drafting a couple of safe, plain‑language options below. I kept legal nuance light but accurate; if procurement wants links to technical safeguards (SCCs, encryption-at-rest, KMS), we can add a short parenthetical or tooltip.

Short option (1 line):
"Redwood processes customer requests to provide hosted AI inference; we transfer only the content you send to the service and use subprocessors (listed below) to operate and secure the service — current list and DPA link available."

Expanded option (1–2 lines):
"We process and store only the customer inputs and outputs necessary to provide hosted inference. Data may be routed to subprocessors (e.g., cloud infra, monitoring, security tools); a current subprocessor list and the full DPA are published at the links below."

Suggested tooltip copy (console - ~2 lines):
"Data handling: we store request/response content to provide inference and optimize performance. See DPA and full subprocessor list for details (updated weekly)."

Notes for procurement: we should not assert countries of transfer in the public snippet; instead link to a DPA summary that explains cross‑border transfers and safeguards (SCCs, encryption, vendor audits). If Globex needs an attestation frequency or SOC2 artifacts, that's a separate evidence request.

I'll leave the tone options open for product to make more concise UI text.

Kimberly
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference



From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Maria Gomez <maria.gomez@globexanalytics.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Oct 2026 15:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick: public copy for DPA/subprocessor list in our procurement packet

Thanks — below are UI‑friendly variants based on Kimberly's language. We kept them short for a console setting (one line) plus a tooltip for additional context. I also attached a small markdown file with copy snippets for procurement and a screenshot suggestion.

Console line (one line):
"We process only the data you send to the API; view our DPA and current subprocessor list for details."

Tooltip (when user hovers the info icon):
"We store request and response content to deliver inference and maintain service quality. Subprocessors (cloud infra, monitoring, security) help operate the service. Subprocessor list updated weekly; DPA link contains transfer and safeguard details."

Procurement packet snippet (slightly expanded):
"Redwood receives and processes the content you send to the hosted API to provide inference. We engage subprocessors (e.g., cloud providers, observability tools) to operate and secure the service; a live subprocessor list and our DPA are linked below."

If Globex prefers a version that mentions the update cadence explicitly, use: "...Subprocessor list (updated weekly) and the full DPA are linked below."

Attached: console_privacy_snippets_v1.md (includes short + expanded options and a one‑click copy link for the procurement doc).

Laura
Principal Product Manager, Console
Redwood Inference

- Mon–Tue (Oct 19–20): 10:00–12:00 PDT / 18:00–20:00 BST
- Wed (Oct 21): 08:30–11:00 PDT / 16:30–19:00 BST
- Thu (Oct 22): 13:00–15:00 PDT / 21:00–23:00 BST

title: Sunset Salon: VIP curated menu & valet ops
preview:
From: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@helixhealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Helix Health Events <events@helixhealth.com>
Date: 2026-09-22T10:15:00-07:00
Subject: Sunset Salon: VIP curated menu & valet ops

Hi Chloe,

Thanks again for hosting the Helix workshop — we'd love to stay on-site for an intimate exec dinner the evening of Oct 14 for ~20 guests (Helix execs + 3 external advisors). Can Redwood help with a seated, curated menu, valet parking, and guest escort/door staffing? Key asks: 

- Arrival window: 6:00–6:30 PM (we'll plan for a staggered arrival)
- Valet: 3 cars at minimum immediately at arrival; an additional overflow lane would be helpful
- Security: two escorts at the entrance during arrival and for seating
- Dietary: we'll send restrictions but expect some vegan and gluten-free needs

Attached is a preliminary VIP list (vip_list.xlsx) with names and provisional arrival ETAs. Can we confirm whether a tasting is needed prior to Oct 14, and what deadline you need for final headcount? Also please confirm the on-site contact and cell number for the evening.

Appreciate it — Olivia

--
Olivia Barnes
Director, Corporate Events | Helix Health
olivia.barnes@helixhealth.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

Attachment: vip_list.xlsx (xlsx, 12 KB)

From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-23T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Sunset Salon: VIP curated menu & valet ops

Hi Olivia — great to hear from you. Excited to host Helix on Oct 14. Quick confirmation and next steps from our side: 

- We can cover valet and door staffing. Logan (copied) will coordinate the vendor.
- We recommend a three-course plated menu with two vegan/gluten-free plated alternates; we can provide sample menus.

title: Re: Urgent — tail latency with long-context requests (32k) impacting Fable Health
preview:
From: Ana Ruiz <ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com>
To: dylan.brooks@redwood.ai
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 15:45:00 -0500
Subject: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Hi Dylan,

Today at ~11:30am PT our production integration started seeing huge tail latency on calls that include long patient-history prompts (~28k–32k tokens). p95 climbed from ~220ms to 3.2s and p99 to 8.7s; error rate spiked briefly to 6%. This is hitting our scheduled summarization jobs and causing timeouts in our pipeline.

We tried reducing concurrency and that helped marginally, but the behavior persists and is intermittent. I’ve attached a short sample of request IDs and timestamps below.

Sample request ids: rwd-ff8c-9a2c, rwd-ff9d-3b1e, rwd-ffa1-4c6b
Approx prompt sizes: 27,900 — 32,100 tokens

This is impacting a critical customer workflow and we need an ETA on root cause and mitigation. Can you escalate?

Thanks,
Ana
Ana Ruiz
Head of Integrations — Fable Health
ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com

Attachments: (log excerpts referenced; full debug requested)

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: Fwd: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Team — forwarding an urgent customer report from Fable Health. They’re seeing p95/p99 regressions and intermittent errors when sending ~32k token requests. Customer impact: production summarization jobs failing/timeouts.

Immediate asks:
- Logan: please triage — can you confirm whether this is a runtime regression or capacity/config issue? Include whether this ties to the kernel changes we rolled last week (v2.3.4).
- Jordan: can you coordinate the customer comms + credit/SLA remediation if we need to offer one?
- Evelyn: requesting leadership awareness; if this looks like a systemic regression we may need exec-level outreach.

I attached the minimal log bundle Fable provided and a short timeline. I’ll stand by on a call if needed.

Attachments: redwood_debug_log_tenant_fable_20261005.tar.gz

—
Dylan Brooks
Senior Account Executive, Hosted — Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 18:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Running quick triage notes — preliminary findings below (summary first):

Summary:
- Root cause (likely): increased frequency of KV-cache misses combined with a fallback to a less-optimized kernel path when sequence length >~24k on a subset of GPU families. This increases compute and memory churn which pushes some requests into higher-latency execution paths.
- Contributing factor: a change in kernel selection logic deployed in v2.3.4 (last week) appears to prefer a new attention kernel that performs well for mid-length sequences but regresses for explicit 32k windows under certain initial KV cache states.
- Repro: repros locally with tenant prompt payloads provided by customer; p99 latency goes from ~600ms to ~8–9s when we force the same kernel fallback.

Immediate mitigations I can take now:
1) Rollback the kernel selection tweak for the hosted pool handling this tenant (risk: slight perf loss for mid-length but prevents long-tail) — ETA 2 hrs for canary + 30m to monitor.
2) Apply a temporary tenant-level throttle for requests with context > 24k (rate-limit config) to reduce concurrent long-window contention — ETA 45m.
3) Push a configuration to enable more aggressive prefix/KV caching for this tenant (reduces cache misses) — ETA 1 hr.

Attached: short trace output and the debug archive Dylan forwarded. I’ll open a SUP ticket and link the traces.

Technical notes:
- Affected nodes: hosted-gpu-pool-7 (A100v4 family), hosted-gpu-pool-3 (A100v3 subset) — memory footprint spikes observed.
- Observed error spike appears to be due to per-request OOM guard trips on the fallback path; errors recover once fallback finished.

I’ll start with a conservative rollback on a small canary ring to validate. Recommend messaging the customer that we have a mitigation plan and ETA ~3hrs for initial rollback/controls.

—
Logan Wright
SRE — Serving Runtime, Redwood Inference

Attachments: repro_traces.txt, sup_4827_link.txt (SUP-4827)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: ana.ruiz@fablehealth.com, support@fablehealth.com
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Hosted API — severe tail latency for 32k prompts (tenant:fable)

Thanks Logan — appreciate the quick triage. Proposed action plan for customer comms & ops:

1) Immediate customer message (I drafted below) — apologize + note mitigations and ETA. Offer a proactive credit if we miss SLA windows.
2) Ops: run rollback canary ASAP and enable tenant throttle + KV cache tuning for Fable.
3) Product follow-up: investigate long-term guardrails — reject/queue extreme-size requests or route to dedicated capacity if they exceed live thresholds.

Draft message to Fable (please edit):
---
Ana — thanks for the report and the logs. We’ve identified a high-confidence mitigation path: we will rollback a kernel-selection change that is increasing tail latency for 24k–32k context lengths, and we will enable tenant-level rate limits for very large prompts while we monitor. We expect the first mitigation (canary rollback + config) within ~3 hours and will update you when the canary is validated. We are prepared to offer a service credit if this impacts your SLAs.
---

Request: Dylan — please send the message to Ana once rollback canary starts and confirm the credit offer details with Sales/Finance. Evelyn — can you sign off on the credit threshold (suggest 10% of this month’s usage for affected pipelines)?

I’ll follow with a short postmortem and product proposal for long-term handling of extreme-length prompts.

—
Jordan Blake
Product — Hosted Inference

title: Demo window reshuffle for Brightline — CTO flight delay
Keystore & Ephemeral SSO - priority sync and scope
preview:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Anita Desai <anita@clearwave.com>, Tom Reeves <tom.reeves@clearwave.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 15:08:00 -0700
Subject: Keystore & Ephemeral SSO - priority sync and scope

Hi Anita / Tom —

Thanks again for the call this morning. I wanted to summarize the asks we noted and propose a short internal triage so we can give you concrete scope and a timeline. Attached is our working draft proposal (redwood-proposal-keystore-ephemeral-v2.pdf) and I included the requirements file you sent for reference.

Customer asks (as I heard them):
- 30-day SSO session logs retention (searchable, exportable).
- BYOK with monthly rotation and ability to provide proof of key custody (rotation history).
- Support for ephemeral session keys (short lived) but with immutable audit trail (who minted key, which model/route used).
- Evidence pack for SOC2 reviewers and procurement (exportable CSV + PDF summary).

Internal ask: can we propose an MVP that addresses the most urgent gating items for ClearWave while keeping implementation scope bounded?

Proposed triage options (please weigh in):
A) MVP-logging: Deliver SSO session logs + 30d retention and CSV export UI. (Low lift, 4–6 weeks)
B) BYOK-lite: Allow customer-supplied keys (manual rotation via Console + rotation events logged). (Medium, 10–12 weeks)
C) Full BYOK + escrow: BYOK with monthly rotation API, rotation proof artifacts, optional escrow for emergency decryption (High, add'l 4–6 weeks).
D) Ephemeral keystore with audit trail: mint short-lived keys, sign mint events into audit ledger (Medium-high, 8–10 weeks).

Next steps I propose: internal triage call today, decide which combination we commit to for ClearWave’s procurement deadline (they asked for timelines by COB Monday).

Attaching: redwood-proposal-keystore-ephemeral-v2.pdf and linking their requirements spreadsheet for easy reference.

Thanks —
Logan
Logan Wright
Head of Product Partnerships | Redwood Inference


From: Anita Desai <anita@clearwave.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Reeves <tom.reeves@clearwave.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 15:32:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Keystore & Ephemeral SSO - priority sync and scope

Logan —

Thanks for the clear summary and for the draft. A few clarifications from our side that affect priority: 
1) SOC2 reviewers will expect 30 days of session-level logs, with the ability to search by user, session id, and tenant id. CSV export is sufficient for now; a UI is nice-to-have.
2) BYOK is a requirement for us. We rotate monthly; we need either a way to push keys and rotation events into Redwood or an escrow arrangement where we can demonstrate the chain-of-custody.
3) Ephemeral keys are acceptable for short-lived tokens, but our compliance team wants an immutable record of key issuance and key association to API calls (audit chain).

Procurement timeline: we need a written scope + delivery estimate by Monday EOD to include in our vendor packet. If you can commit to an MVP that covers (1) session logs & 30d retention and (2) BYOK-lite (manual rotation + rotation audit), that will unblock procurement. Full BYOK API and escrow can be treated as a follow-up feature.

I attached the requirements matrix (ClearWave_requirements_v1.xlsx) — there are some fields you may not need right away. Happy to hop on a quick call if helpful.

Best,
Anita Desai
Head of Security Operations | ClearWave Health


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 16:01:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Keystore & Ephemeral SSO - priority sync and scope

+1 to Anita's ask for an MVP that covers logs + BYOK-lite. A few technical notes / risks for consideration:
- SSO session logs: we currently capture SSO assertions and token exchange events in telemetry but not in a customer-exportable audit table. Building an export path + retention policy is straightforward (schema + pipeline) but we need to consider PII minimization and redaction rules.
- BYOK-lite: supporting customer-supplied key material on the Console is doable fast, but we must ensure KV access controls and that rotation events are immutably timestamped. Vault integration (AWS KMS + customer-key metadata) will be the safe path.
- Ephemeral keys + signed mint events: this requires either a hardened signing-issuer service or use of a HSM to sign mint records so they can't be tampered with. That adds complexity (HSM provisioning, KMS policies).

Questions for the product team:
1) Do we want to support key upload + manual rotation in Console as the MVP, or require API-driven rotation from day one?
2) What retention SLA should we promise for evidence exports (e.g., how quickly can we produce CSV extracts for an auditor)?

I can draft an internal implementation checklist if we agree the MVP scope.

-ben
Ben Carter
Senior Security Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 16:34:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Keystore & Ephemeral SSO - priority sync and scope

Thanks Ben — this is helpful. Quick product constraints and a suggested phased roadmap: 
Phase 0 (planning): align on data schema for audit events, redaction policy, and acceptance criteria for CSV exports. (1 week)
Phase 1 (MVP - 4–6 weeks): implement customer-exportable SSO session audit table, retention policy = 30d, CSV export endpoint + Console link. This satisfies the procurement blocker.
Phase 2 (BYOK-lite - 8–10 weeks): Console key upload, manual rotation UI, rotation-event logging (immutable events). No escrow in this phase.
Phase 3 (BYOK-API + Escrow - 12–16 weeks): API for rotation, rotation proofs, escrow options and legal flow.
Phase 4 (Ephemeral keystore + signed audit ledger - 14–18 weeks): hardened signing service / HSM, signed mint events, ledger export for auditors.

To Ben's question: MVP should be Console upload + manual rotation for BYOK-lite — that reduces API surface and shortens delivery time. Evidence export SLA: target 48 hours for assembled CSV + PDF summary for an auditor (we can improve after).

Irene
Irene Choi
Director, Product (Security) | Redwood Inference

Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 17:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Keystore & Ephemeral SSO - priority sync and scope

Anita / Tom —

Appreciate the quick turnaround. Internal triage results: we will commit to the following plan for ClearWave’s procurement packet:
- Deliverable A (MVP, 4–6 weeks): SSO session audit table + 30d retention, Console CSV export and UI for search by user/session/tenant. Evidence export SLA: 48 hours.
- Deliverable B (BYOK-lite, 8–10 weeks): Console key upload + manual rotation UI, rotation events recorded immutably in audit table.
- Follow-up (BYOK-API + Escrow + Ephemeral audit ledger): scoped as roadmap items with target windows (12–18 weeks) — we will provide estimates and a separate SOW if you need them sooner.

Next steps for procurement: I’ll send a short scope doc and timeline (linked) and Ben will own the implementation checklist. Please confirm if the MVP items above are sufficient for procurement and share any redlines to the draft proposal by Monday EOD.

Logan

Head of Product Partnerships | Redwood Inference

title: Hydra AI capacity scenario walkthrough and next steps
preview:
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Alex Wu <alex.wu@hydraai.com>
Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Hydra AI — weekly fleet planning kickoff (scenarios)

Team — starting this thread to align on capacity scenarios for Hydra AI. They closed lead pricing last week and asked for a committed Dedicated pool proposal + burst plan. HubSpot shows a close window in Q4 which could ramp to sustained 2M tokens/day if the pilot goes well.

Key asks from Sales/Finance:
- Provide three capacity scenarios (Conservative, Base, Aggressive) with GPU slot counts, expected latency SLOs, and monthly cost estimate.
- Identify which scenarios require reserved capacity vs burst credits.
- Highlight any lead time risks (hardware, region) and fallback model options.

Logan — can you start with a short sketch of the infrastructure implications? Kimberly/Ben — I'll need a suggested pricing carve for dedicated slots and a short note for procurement cadence.

I attached a simple demand workbook Hydra shared (Hydra_MATP_v2.xlsx) and a draft capacity model. Please annotate in-sheet if helpful.

Thanks — Laura
Laura Bennett
Senior Sales Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: Hydra_MATP_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet)


From: Alex Wu <alex.wu@hydraai.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@hydraai.com
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 10:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hydra AI — weekly fleet planning kickoff (scenarios)

Laura — thanks for the ping. Quick clarifying notes from our side:
- Pilot target: 200k tokens/day for 6 weeks (evaluation). Production estimate if pilot succeeds: 2M tokens/day steady state by month 3.
- Latency target: p95 <= 500ms for single-turn API usage. Some batch ingest will tolerate higher latency.
- Region: US-west primarily, with a small EU footprint later (not in initial commit).

Procurement: procurement@hydraai.com would prefer a 12-month reserved commitment but open to a 6+6 contract if we can demonstrate predictable cost. We can accelerate a signed SOW if pricing aligns.

Happy to hop on a short call Tuesday to walk through numbers.

Best,
Alex
Head of Platform Partnerships
Hydra AI


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Alex Wu <alex.wu@hydraai.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 12:18:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Hydra AI — weekly fleet planning kickoff (scenarios)

Ben/Laura — I sketched three infrastructure scenarios in the attached capacity-model-v3.csv (high level):

1) Conservative (pilot-first)
- Dedicated: 2 x A100-80GB slots (reserved for pilot windows), burst available up to +2 slots on demand.
- Expected throughput: ~250k tokens/day sustained across the pool.
- Latency: p95 ~ 350–450ms (depends on batching and prefix cache).
- Lead time: minimal — use existing US-west pool with cold-start allowance. No new hardware procurement.
- Notes: Works for Hydra's pilot. If they exceed pilot, we'd need to transition to Base.

2) Base (target production commit)
- Dedicated: 8 x A100-80GB reserved. Burst: +6 slots (short-term burst credits).
- Expected throughput: ~1.8–2.2M tokens/day depending on prompt length.
- Latency: p95 ~ 300–420ms with optimized batching and KV cache.
- Lead time: 4–6 week provisioning if we need to expand reserved pool beyond current capacity in the region.
- Notes: Balanced cost/latency; recommended baseline for a 12-month commit.

3) Aggressive (fast growth hedge)
- Dedicated: 12 x A100-80GB reserved + 8 x mixed-quantized T4/RTX burst slots for lower-cost inference on background jobs.
- Expected throughput: 3.5–4M tokens/day with cost-optimized routing.
- Latency: p95 ~ 280–380ms for main model; background tasks slower.
- Lead time: up to 8–12 weeks to lock in additional reserved hardware in US-west if we need new racks.
- Notes: Best for accounts pushing multi-region or low-latency SLAs.

Cost roughs (monthly committed GPU cost, ballpark):
- Conservative: $45k/month
- Base: $165k/month (12-month reserved pricing assumed)
- Aggressive: $270k/month

Risks and mitigations:
- Spike risk: If Hydra unexpectedly hits +50% token volume, burst quota may be exhausted — fallback to lower-capacity model variants and tightened batching.
- Warranty/Procurement: New reserved slots need formal finance approval; provisioning timeline depends on available vendor inventory.

I flagged the exact per-slot run-rate in capacity-model-v3.csv. Happy to walk through the math on a call.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Head of Infra Planning
Redwood Inference

--

title: FinBridge live chat: intermittent streaming lag and short-term fixes
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Turner <alice.turner@finbridge.com>, FinBridge SRE <ops@finbridge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: FinBridge live chat: intermittent streaming lag and short-term fixes

Hi Alice / FinBridge SRE team,

Heads-up: our telemetry flagged intermittent high tail-latency on streaming/chat responses for your production org starting ~08:50 PDT this morning and recurring in short bursts (roughly 15-45s degradation windows). We see the error pattern on the streaming channel only — non-streaming generate endpoints are not experiencing the same token-level delays.

Immediate items we've executed:
- Scoped affected paths to the streaming websocket/proxy layer and a small set of edge shards (attached excerpt).
- Rolled a targeted restart of the suspect edge pool to clear a connection backlog (completed at 09:02 PDT).

Recommended short-term mitigations for your app to minimize user impact until we fully remediate:
1) Fallback: switch to short-polling or single-shot generate for critical flows when stream latency exceeds 5s.
2) Client timeouts: set a 12s client-side streaming inactivity timeout with a retry backoff (linear 2s x 2 attempts).
3) Model variant: if acceptable, route critical requests to the service’s non-streaming endpoint using the same model variant — this preserves throughput at slightly higher cost.

I've attached an incident_log_excerpt.txt with timestamps and correlation ids to help your SREs correlate. Can you share any additional client-side logs (connection ids, approximate user impact windows) — if you’re okay with it, we may request a short packet capture for one impacted session to compare timing at your edge vs ours.

We’ll keep you updated every 2 hours or on any material change. Sorry for the disruption — working on the root-cause analysis now.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0162

Attachment: incident_log_excerpt.txt (text)

From: Alice Turner <alice.turner@finbridge.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: FinBridge SRE <ops@finbridge.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 09:34:21 -0700
Subject: Re: FinBridge live chat: intermittent streaming lag and short-term fixes

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for the quick heads up. We saw a spike in user complaints starting ~08:55 and a handful of sessions that timed out in our UI. Customer impact was limited to web chat; mobile clients appear less affected (uncertain if due to traffic patterns or different client timeouts).

We can implement the client-side timeout and fallback you suggested — ETA for a patch to the web UI is ~3 hours.

Question: is there an option on your side to divert our traffic away from streaming to a non-streaming path automatically during these windows? That would be the fastest mitigation for us.

I'll share a packet capture from one affected session (ops@finbridge will send) and attach our front-end logs. Please keep us posted.

Best,
Alice Turner
Product Lead, FinBridge
alice.turner@finbridge.com

> On Thu, Sep 10, 2026 at 09:12, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Heads-up: our telemetry flagged intermittent high tail-latency on streaming/chat responses for your production org...

Attachment: finbridge_frontend_logs_09-10.txt (sent separately)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 10:05:12 -0700
Subject: Fwd: FinBridge live chat: intermittent streaming lag and short-term fixes

Marissa / Logan — looped you both for visibility and quick ops coordination for FinBridge (high priority customer, production chat impact). Summary: streaming-only token emission delays, bursts of high latency at edge/proxy layer; FinBridge intends to implement client-side fallback in ~3h and sent us a packet capture.

Preliminary theory from our on-call infra team: a memory pressure regression in the shard orchestration path caused connection pooling exhaustion on the streaming proxy for a small subset of edge hosts. Symptoms: long GC pauses + backlog of in-flight KV cache requests producing serialized token emission. We saw the following evidence in the logs attached: repeated \"proxy-send-timeout\" for 3 edge hosts, spike in ephemeral socket CLOSE_WAIT count, and sustained increase in per-connection latency before we restarted the pool.

Short-term ops plan (we should action now):
- Increase stream proxy connection pool cap for FinBridge traffic by 30% (temporary config; no deploy).
- Force a rolling restart of the 3 affected edge hosts this morning (already scheduled, rolling).
- Enable a temporary per-tenant rate limiter at the mid-proxy to prevent burstback when connections re-establish.
- Prepare a concise customer comms + proposed credit offer (we should include clear ETA and a link to the postmortem draft).

I'll attach a packet trace sample we requested and the redwood_postmortem_draft (internal) — please review edits inline and confirm the customer-facing wording.

Thanks,
Karthik

Attachment: network_trace_sample.pcap (pcap), redwood_postmortem_draft.pdf (internal draft)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Turner <alice.turner@finbridge.com>, FinBridge SRE <ops@finbridge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 11 Sep 2026 14:55:00 -0700
Subject: FinBridge live chat: final update, remediation steps, and postmortem draft

Hi Alice / ops team — quick final update and next steps. Thank you for the packet capture and logs; they were helpful to correlate with our traces.

What we did:
- Completed rolling restart of the three affected edge hosts (09:02 -> 10:10 PDT on 9/10).
- Increased connection pool cap for FinBridge routing and enabled temporary per-tenant rate limiting (9/10 10:12 PDT).
- Deployed a small GC tuning patch to the shard orchestration service (9/10 12:05 PDT) to reduce pause durations.

Result: the streaming latency bursts ceased after the restart + config changes; we observed normal token emission rates from 12:20 PDT onward. We’ll continue 72-hour increased telemetry retention for your org and proactively monitor for regressions.

Compensation: we propose a one-time service credit equal to 10% of September usage for FinBridge, applied to your next billing cycle. If that’s not appropriate, we can discuss an alternative.

Next: attached is a draft postmortem (internal link above and attached PDF) with timeline, root cause, and long-term mitigations (automated shard recycling, improved connection pool self-heal, and faster GC telemetry alerts). Please review and tell us if you want anything changed before we publish the customer-facing summary. We plan to publish a polished customer postmortem to your team by 09/13 EOD PT.

Apologies again for the disruption — appreciate your patience and the quick collaboration from your SREs.

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0162

----
Redwood Inference — Production Support & Accounts
Attachment: redwood_postmortem_draft.pdf (internal draft)

Notes (2 bullets):
