title: Renewal: term amendment + EU residency + APAC pilot and cross-region failover
preview:
From: Sara Liu <sara.liu@northbridgehealth.org>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: procurements@northbridgehealth.org
Date: 2026-08-02T09:12:00Z
Subject: Renewal questions — EU residency + APAC pilot and failover

Hi Logan / Marissa,

Thanks for the renewal notice. A couple of items are blocking signoff from our side that I wanted to raise early:

1) EU residency: our regulators require that production patient-derived inference for EU customers stays in EU data centers. We need an explicit contractual residency clause and a short runbook for onboarding.
2) APAC pilot: we want to run a 3-month APAC pilot for our Singapore tenants. This will require a pilot deployment in APAC region with dedicated capacity for peak hours (est. 80 p50 requests/sec) and the ability to burst to shared capacity.
3) Cross-region failover: we need a documented failover plan — if APAC is unavailable, traffic should fail over to EU with clear data handling and agreed RTO/RPO.

Can you confirm whether these can be captured in the renewal amendment and any cost delta? Also please include any compliance evidence (SOC 2) and proposed timelines.

Regards,
Sara Liu
Head of Platform, Northbridge Health

---
Attachments: NBH_Renewal_Questions_20260802.docx (docx)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Sara Liu <sara.liu@northbridgehealth.org>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-02T12:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Renewal questions — EU residency + APAC pilot and failover

Hi Sara — thanks for the clear list. Quick summary of what we can do and next steps: 

- EU residency clause: we can add an amendment that requires production inference for EU tenant data to be served from our EU-hosted capacity (datacenter locations listed by region). We also include KMS scope and audit logs access. Draft clause attached.

- APAC pilot: propose a 3-month pilot on Redwood Dedicated burst pool in APAC (1 reserved node + burst). Estimated incremental monthly commit: $12,500 for reserved capacity + metered token usage. We'll send a SOW and pilot runbook.

- Cross-region failover: technical failover is supported — we use smart routing with policy-based fallbacks. There are two important legal items: (1) temporary cross-border processing allowed during active failover windows, and (2) explicit SLA for failover RTO (we typically propose 30 minutes) and notification procedures.

I'll loop in Karthik to map the operational timeline and Monica for procurement terms. Can we schedule a 45-min call tomorrow to walk through?

Best,
Logan Wright
Director, Strategic Accounts — Redwood Inference

Attachments: NBH_SOW_Q3_2026_draft.pdf (pdf)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Liu <sara.liu@northbridgehealth.org>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-03T07:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Renewal — technical plan and timelines

Logan / team / Sara,

Notes from our infra side — ops-impact and timeline: 

- EU residency: we can provision EU-only pools in 2 weeks after contract amendment sign-off. This includes KMS integration (customer-managed keys supported via KMS connector) and audit log delivery to a dedicated S3 in the EU.

- APAC pilot sizing: for the 80 p50 steady state, we recommend 1 dedicated A100-equivalent node with auto-burst to shared fleet. Warm-up and KV-cache priming will require a 48-hour pre-cutover traffic ramp.

- Failover behavior: routing policy will route APAC traffic to EU only when APAC health crosses the configured threshold. During failover, we will mask tenant identifiers for logging until an ops window is opened (to satisfy residency controls) — we need legal signoff on that approach.

Action items:
- Legal to review cross-border processing language for failover (Logan).
- Commercial to confirm pilot pricing and term amendment (Marissa).
- Ops to produce a short runbook and a pre-cutover checklist (Karthik / Monica).

I'll attach a diagram for the APAC pilot architecture.

-Karthik

Attachments: APAC-pilot-architecture-20260803.png (png)

From: Sara Liu <sara.liu@northbridgehealth.org>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: procurements@northbridgehealth.org, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-04T14:20:00Z
Subject: Re: Renewal — clarifications and procurement timeline

Thanks Karthik — helpful. A few clarifications from procurement and security: 

- The procurement team needs a redlined amendment that includes: residency clause, KMS responsibilities, failover cross-border clause limited to declared incident windows, and data deletion obligations after failover. Can you provide that by EOD Friday?
- Security would like our SOC 2 evidence and a short description of the audit log schema delivered to us.
- APAC pilot: procurement will authorize the pilot as a separate SOW if the monthly commit does not exceed $15k. Please confirm the final number including expected metered charges.

We are targeting signature the week of Aug 16 if redline and SOW are in place.

-- Sara

Attachments: SOC2-evidence-redwood-2026.pdf (pdf)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Sara Liu <sara.liu@northbridgehealth.org>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, procurement@northbridgehealth.org
Date: 2026-08-06T17:43:00Z
Subject: Re: Renewal — proposed amendment and next steps

Sara / team — attaching the redline and a short SOW for the APAC pilot. Summary of commitments: 

- Residency amendment: production inference for EU tenants will be served from EU-only pools. Redwood provides KMS connector; keys remain customer-managed. Audit logs exported to EU S3 within 24 hours.
- Failover clause: agreed temporary cross-border processing allowed for declared failover incidents; RTO 30 minutes; Redwood to notify within 10 minutes of failover initiation. Data deletion after failover will follow the attached runbook.
- APAC pilot SOW: 3 months, reserved baseline $11,500/month + metered usage estimates. Pilot includes a 48-hour warm-up ramp and a single smoke test cutover window.

Next steps:
- Sara to route the redline to legal — we request redline back by Aug 11 to hit the Aug 16 signature target.
- Procurement to confirm PO approval and finalize the minimal monthly commit figure.
- Karthik / Monica to schedule an ops onboarding session for Aug 19 (pre-cutover ramp).

Thanks — we’ll be on the call to walk through the runbook if helpful.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

Attachments: NBH_renewal_amendment_redline_20260806.pdf (pdf), APAC-pilot-SOW-20260806.pdf (pdf)

title: Initial MAP kickoff — Mediapulse evaluation plan & timeline
preview:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Emma Holtz <emma.holtz@mediapulse.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Initial MAP kickoff — Mediapulse evaluation plan & timeline

Hi Emma —

Great to meet you last week. As discussed, I wanted to kick off a short Mutual Action Plan (MAP) so we’re aligned on goals, key stakeholders, and a target close window. I attached a brief MAP template to make this easier to iterate on.

High-level proposed goals from our side:
- 2-week technical evaluation with SSO + sample dataset
- Performance benchmark (latency & cost) on 10k daily inference volume
- Security checklist verification (SOC2 evidence + retention policy)

Proposed timeline (flexible):
1) Week 1 (Aug 10–14): access + basic validation; SSO + test corpus ingestion
2) Week 2 (Aug 17–21): workload run & perf metrics; initial quality review
3) Week 3 (Aug 24–28): final review, procurement/PO conversation, and readiness to sign

Who I have on the internal side: me (AE) and Karthik (solutions engineer) — we’ll bring Priya (customer success) into the onboarding/pre-sales ops conversations as we get dates locked. Can you confirm the champion and any additional stakeholders we should include (security, procurement, platform)? Also please confirm if the timeline above looks reasonable to you or if you’d prefer a later window.

Attachment: Mediapulse_MAP_Template.xlsx (Excel, 15 KB)

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0172

title: Action required: Clean up stale opportunities & finalize commit for weekly forecast
preview:
From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>; Sales Team <sales@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 1 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Stale opps export + request to update stages/close dates before Monday forecast

Hi team,

Attached is a quick export of opportunities assigned to the West & Strategic segments that haven't been touched in 60+ days. Finance asked us to push a cleaner commit on Monday's forecast call — they want any opportunity without a verified close date or a recent rep comment to be either:

- updated with a realistic close date, or
- advanced/dropped to the correct stage, or
- marked as no-go/closed-lost with reason.

Please prioritize deals with TPV > $50k (column: ARR_estimate) and any deals in Negotiation/Contracting. I highlighted those in the CSV.

Next steps:
1) Review the attached stale_pipeline_export_20260801.csv by EOD Monday.
2) If you plan to keep an opp in commit, add a 1–2 line comment in the hubspot note and set a verified close date.
3) If it's gone dark and you can't contact the buyer, change the stage to "No Response" and add a reason.

I'll roll up a cleaned forecast and send to Finance before the Tuesday sync. CC'ing Marissa to flag any questions.

Attachments: stale_pipeline_export_20260801.csv (CSV, 52 rows)

Thanks,
Rachel
Rachel Kim
Sales Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

Logan — appreciate you jumping on this. A few clarifications from Finance's perspective before you lock commits:

- For any opp > $100k, we need either a signed LOI/PO or a confirmed close date with a named buyer and procurement timeline. If you have a doc, drop the DocuSign link in the opportunity.
- If you've had a demo or legal exchange but no calendar activity in 30 days, assume it's stale and move out of commit unless you have an active next step scheduled.
- Pull vs push: If you're expecting a partner to close by carrier timing (e.g., channel-led) but you can't name the buyer, do not pull into commit — push it to Best Case and add a reason.

Rachel — please include the 90+ day stale count in the rollup (number of opps and total ARR). Finance will use that in variance analysis.

Logan — I need your final commit list by COB today so we can finalize the consolidated forecast for Tuesday's review. If you need help triaging, loop in your AE peers and mark any borderline opps with "review-needed" tag in HubSpot.

Marissa

Redwood Inference

title: Handoff: prefix/KV-cache plan + model shortlist and token forecast for NexaHealth
preview:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Handoff: prefix/KV-cache plan + model shortlist and token forecast for NexaHealth

Team — handing off NexaHealth onboarding (deal ND-2026-1458). High-level summary and asks below; I've attached the token forecast, the raw shortlist, and an initial KV/prefix cache draft. Please review and propose final model & infra recommendations by EOD Mon so we can lock the implementation sprint.

What this customer is building
- Customer: NexaHealth (patient triage + physician follow-up assistant)
- Primary product: conversational assistant that ingests patient messages, chart snippets, and past conversation -> generates suggested triage and templates for clinicians.
- Context needs: many conversations reuse a stable patient history + last 6 messages; p95 context length ~6k tokens, occasional long-context chart uploads that we plan to store externally and pass via references.

Traffic & token snapshot (see spreadsheet for full calc)
- DAU (expected ramp): 2,000 (steady-state target month)
- Avg requests per active user/day: 2 -> ~120k requests/month
- Avg prompt tokens (incl. patient history snippets + system instructions): 420 tokens
- Avg generated tokens: 150 tokens
- Monthly prompt tokens: ~50.4M; monthly generated tokens: ~18M; total ~68.4M tokens/month
- Peak profile: p95 burst ~3x baseline traffic; expect short peaks where we need headroom for ~40 req/s sustained bursts for minutes.

Model shortlist (initial evaluation)
- Mistral-7B-v0 (quantize to 4-bit): best cost / latency tradeoff for short->medium context; recommend for baseline throughput testing.
- Llama-3-8B-instruct (8k context): low-latency inference path; strong for instruction following on clinical prompts.
- Falcon-40B-instruct (16k context variant candidate): higher quality on long, structured outputs — expensive but useful for fallback on complex chart-summary tasks.

Operational considerations & asks
1) Prefix/KV-cache: customer will have many recurring patients; we should provision a prefix cache (KV) per patient to avoid re-sending full patient history each request. Draft attached. Need engineering estimating cost delta and cache invalidation semantics.
2) Batching / autoscale policy: p95 burst multiplier + fast scale-up window (target spin-up < 60s). Recommend a small warm pool for steady baseline and burst autoscale for p95 events.
3) Quantization profile: test 4-bit for Mistral and Llama-3 8B; keep Falcon-40B to int8 for first pass.
4) SLOs: target 95th percentile latency <= 800ms for short requests (<=1k tokens) on baseline. Longer/complex requests can be queued to higher-cost nodes.

Deliverables requested from eng/ops by Monday EOD
- Ben: rough GPU-hour and cost sketch for steady-state + p95 burst (include warm pool sizing).
- Grace: perf plan to load-test KV cache hit rates (simulate returning patients) and expected token savings.
- Monica: integration checklist (data flow, KMS keys, retention window) for Private/Dedicated options.

Attachments: nex a_token_forecast.xlsx, model_shortlist_and_notes.csv, kv-cache-design-draft.pdf

Thanks — I’ll be on the handoff call Friday 10:00 PT to walk through the workbook.

— Logan
Logan Wright
Sr. Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Jul 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Handoff: prefix/KV-cache plan + model shortlist and token forecast for NexaHealth

Thanks Logan — quick clarifying questions before I start designing the KV cache perf tests.

On Thu, 22 Jul 2026 at 09:12, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — handing off NexaHealth onboarding (deal ND-2026-1458). High-level summary and asks below; I've attached the token forecast, the raw shortlist, and an initial KV/prefix cache draft. Please review and propose final model & infra recommendations by EOD Mon so we can lock the implementation sprint.
>
(quoted for context)

Questions:
1) Patient history TTL: do they expect updates to history to be reflected immediately? If TTL is long then cache staleness is a concern.
2) Long chart uploads: will those be stored as external artifacts with a stable URL we can dereference during inference, or do they want the text inlined into prompts?
3) Expected % of returning vs new conversations? Our token savings hinge on at least ~60% return rate to break even on cache ops.

I’ll start drafting test harness that simulates a 65/35 returning/new split with p95 bursts; that should give us numbers we can feed back to Ben.

— Grace
Grace O'Connor
Principal Performance Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Jul 2026 15:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Handoff: prefix/KV-cache plan + model shortlist and token forecast for NexaHealth

Adding compute/ops perspective — rough back-of-envelope below. I’ll produce a spreadsheet with GPU-hours tomorrow.

On Thu, 22 Jul 2026 at 11:03, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Logan — quick clarifying questions before I start designing the KV cache perf tests.

Assumptions I used: baseline monthly requests 120k (as Logan noted), avg total tokens/request ~570. For steady-state sizing assume 10% of requests happen in a 10-minute peak window; for p95 bursts multiply baseline qps by 3.

Recommendation summary:
- Baseline warm pool: 2 x A10p-equivalent nodes (good for Mistral-7B 4-bit & Llama-3-8B low-latency paths)
- Burst headroom: autoscale up to 8 nodes to cover 3x bursts. Warm pool reduces cold-start P99.
- Estimated steady GPU consumption: ~420 GPU-hours/month (baseline + typical autoscale churn); add ~120 GPU-hours/month for p95 bursts headroom depending on burst frequency.
- Cost delta if we enable prefix/KV caching aggressively: expect ~28–42% token-cost reduction in steady-state (depends on returning rate and average patient history size).

Open items for Nexa decision:
- Warm pool budget cap (customer approval needed)
- Whether Falcon-40B will be supported for chart-heavy requests (we should keep it as a fallback to limit cost).

I’ll post the detailed GPU-hour sheet by 07/23 EOD.

— Ben
Ben Carter
Infra & Cost Modeling Lead | Redwood Inference


From: Aisha Summers <aisha@nexahealth.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@nexahealth.com
Date: Fri, 23 Jul 2026 10:41:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Handoff: prefix/KV-cache plan + model shortlist and token forecast for NexaHealth

Hi Logan / team — thanks for the clear summary and the workbook. A few quick clarifications from our side to help you finalize the sizing: 

- History updates: patient history should be near-real-time; if cache TTL is longer than 30s it will cause clinical mismatches. We can emit an update event stream to invalidate patient prefix entries.
- Chart artifacts: we plan to store structured chart extracts in our S3 and provide a short summary text to the model; full chart text won't be inlined on every request.
- Returning vs new: based on our analytics, ~70% of interactions are with returning patients within a 48-hour window.
- Ramp profile: month 1 target is 500 DAU, month 4 target 2,000 DAU. We expect burst events tied to morning clinician shifts (08:00–10:00 PT).

One ask: can you include an evaluation run that measures savings when prefix caching + short-term TTL (30s–2min) are used vs a no-cache baseline? That will help us justify warm pool spend to our procurement team.

We’re available for the handoff call Friday; if you want to run the perf tests against a representative sample dataset I can provision access tokens.

Best,
Aisha Summers
Head of Product | NexaHealth


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Summers <aisha@nexahealth.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 24 Jul 2026 16:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Handoff: prefix/KV-cache plan + model shortlist and token forecast for NexaHealth

Thanks Aisha — that helps a lot. Quick summary of next actions and owners (we'll cover on the handoff call):

- Grace: run cache-savings perf harness with returning/new split 70/30 and TTL sweep (30s, 2min, 10min). Deliverable: expected token savings matrix + hit-rate curves. Target: 07/28.
- Ben: deliver detailed GPU-hour & warm-pool costing sheet (baseline + p95 scenarios). Include a Falcon-40B fallback cost line. Target: 07/23 EOD.
- Engineering (Logan + Tessa): implement a simple event-driven cache invalidation proof-of-concept and a starter set of integration docs for Nexa S3 artifact dereference (we'll use the drive doc link). Target: demo for Friday call.
- Procurement notes: Monica to start preparing a short justification note to pair with the cost sheet to present to Nexa procurement.

We will aim to run initial model evals with: Mistral-7B (4-bit), Llama-3-8B (8k), Falcon-40B (fallback). If performance shows Mistral meets quality bar on core flows we will propose it as default to optimize unit cost.

Attachments referenced: nex a_token_forecast.xlsx, kv-cache-design-draft.pdf

See you on the handoff call Friday 10:00 PT.

— Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference

title: Cartology trial: proposed success metrics, eval split, and next steps
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Jamie Torres <jamie.torres@cartology.co>, Priya Anand <priya.anand@cartology.co>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-20T16:05:00-07:00
Subject: Cartology trial: proposed success metrics, eval split, and next steps

Hi Jamie / Priya — thanks again for a productive discovery today. Quick recap plus a concrete proposal so we can lock the scope and calendar for the trial.

TL;DR proposal (please confirm):
- Hybrid evaluation: 1 week offline evaluation on a labeled seed (sanitized) dataset + 3 weeks online shadow against live traffic (shadowing only; no production writes).
- Success criteria (pass/fail gates):
  - Business KPIs: +5% engagement lift on recommended items (primary), <2% erroneous recommendations flagged by ops (secondary).
  - Model quality: weighted score >= 78/100 on our rubric (precision/recall + human relevance grading).

title: FinWorks hosted pilot — quickstart checklist, endpoint partitioning & rate plan
preview:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@finworks.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 15:13:00 -0700
Subject: FinWorks hosted pilot — quickstart checklist, endpoint partitioning & rate plan

Hi Elena — thanks again for the demo today, and for walking us through the FinWorks fraud-review flows. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we can size a hosted pilot that aligns with your latency and throughput needs.

What we heard today (summary):
- Primary use: synchronous risk scoring and bot-detection enrichment called from your fraud pipeline (~80% of calls, 120ms target p50).
- Secondary: async batch re-score for nightly reconciliation (lower SLO).
- Expected pilot traffic: 1k TPS peak during business hours, average 150rps; message size ~300 tokens in/out.
- Data residency: US-only; no on-prem requirement in pilot phase.

Questions we need answered to finalize the hosted pilot proposal:
1) Endpoint partitioning: do you want separate endpoints for sync (low-latency) vs async (batch) or a single endpoint with traffic routing?
2) Burst tolerance: confirm acceptable error/retry policy for brief bursts above 1k TPS.
3) Auth & keys: will you use a single service key for the integration or per-environment keys (dev/staging/prod)?

Immediate next steps I propose:
- Kimberly will send a short quickstart checklist and a rate-plan spreadsheet for hosted pilot capacity.
- If hosted looks good, we’ll schedule a 60-min tech kickoff (engineers + infra) to validate network/IP allowlists and test keys.

Attached: redwood_quickstart_checklist.pdf (summary checklist) and a placeholder rate plan.

Timing: if we get your answers this week we can provision a sandbox hosted endpoint by next Tue (7/28) for smoke tests.

Thanks — looking forward to next steps.

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

---
(Attachment stubs: redwood_quickstart_checklist.pdf (1.1MB), finworks-pilot-rateplan-proposal.xlsx (120KB))

From: Elena Soto <elena.soto@finworks.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: FinWorks Engineering Team <eng@finworks.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 09:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FinWorks hosted pilot — quickstart checklist, endpoint partitioning & rate plan

Thanks Logan — really helpful recap. A few clarifications from our side: 

1) Endpoint partitioning: prefer separate endpoints. Our sync path is latency-sensitive and we want a hardened SLA; batch can tolerate higher latency and should be routed to a different endpoint.

2) Burst tolerance: transient spikes happen during promotion windows (up to 2.5k TPS for ~30s). We use exponential backoff client-side but would like an explicit burst buffer or graceful degradation plan rather than hard 429s.

3) Keys: we'll use per-environment keys. Staging team prefers a separate key so they can test without touching prod.

Also: can you confirm whether per-endpoint rate limits are configurable per API key, and whether prefix/KV caching is applicable for our use case? We saw caching mentioned in the product brief but want concrete limits/expectations.

We can be available for a 60-min tech kickoff on Fri (7/24) morning PT if that works for you and your engineers.

Elena
Elena Soto
Head of Product — FinWorks

On Tue, Jul 21, 2026 at 3:13 PM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena — thanks again for the demo today, and for walking us through the FinWorks fraud-review flows. Quick recap and a couple of asks so we can size a hosted pilot that aligns with your latency and throughput needs.
>
> ...


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@finworks.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 11:47:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: FinWorks hosted pilot — quickstart checklist, endpoint partitioning & rate plan

Elena — thanks, good notes. Short answers and proposal from the ops side: 

- Separate endpoints: agreed. We'll provision two hosted endpoints for the pilot (sync.lowlatency.finworks.redwoodapi.com and async.batch.finworks.redwoodapi.com). Sync endpoint will be placed on a lower-concurrency path with quicker preemption and tighter node selection to meet your 120ms p50 target.

- Burst handling: we support configured burst windows and a soft-queueing policy. For your described pattern (2.5k TPS for 30s), we recommend:
  * Pilot burst buffer: 2x baseline capacity for 60s with a 95th-percentile throttling fallback to compatible smaller model variants (auto-fallback).
  * Client-side backoff + server soft-queue reduces 429s; we'll instrument early metrics so you can see queue depth.

- Per-key limits: yes, rate limits can be mapped to API keys and routes. We'll deliver a default rate-band proposal and you can request adjustments before pilot. Prefix/KV caching: applicable — if your requests share common prefixes or repeated context (session-level), caching will cut token costs substantially; we’ll include recommended cache TTLs in the checklist.

Attached is the quickstart checklist and a draft rate-plan (pilot numbers populated from your estimates). Please review columns marked ACTION REQUIRED.

Proposed next actions:
1) FinWorks confirms endpoint partitioning and burst policy acceptance (reply to this thread).
2) FinWorks provides a staging API key scope and egress allowlist by Thu 7/23 EOD PT.
3) Kickoff: Fri 7/24 0900 PT — 60 min (Logan will send invite).

If you want, Ben can run a short architecture deep-dive during the kickoff on model fallback behavior and KV cache impacts.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Solutions Engineer — Redwood Inference

(Attachment stubs: redwood_quickstart_checklist.pdf, finworks-pilot-rateplan-proposal.xlsx)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@finworks.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Jul 2026 09:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FinWorks hosted pilot — quickstart checklist, endpoint partitioning & rate plan

Perfect — thanks for the quick turnaround, Elena.

We’ll proceed with the plan Kimberly outlined. Action items for this thread: 
- Elena / FinWorks: confirm staging API key name and send egress allowlist by Thu 7/23 EOD PT.
- Redwood (Kimberly/Ben): provision the two hosted endpoints and enable the temporary burst buffer.
- Logan: calendar invite sent for Fri 7/24 0900 PT (tech kickoff).

I just sent the invite — please accept when you can. Looking forward to the kickoff; Ben, please be ready to demo fallback & cache metrics.

Cheers,
Logan
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

A few specifics from our side:
- PO number: 7821 (attached)
- Preferred initial cadence: monthly in arrears
- VAT number: ESB12345678 (Spain)

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@horizonhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 09:44:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Invoice and billing setup for PO 7821

Hi Marta — thanks for the details and the PO. Quick answers below and a plan for the first invoice: 

- Delivery modes: we support both approaches. Our default is portal-first (AP push + email copy), but we can also do email-only if you prefer. For most customers the portal plus an email copy reduces manual AP intake errors.

Hi Elena — glad to hear you're ready to start. We can support a time-limited invoice-only path for small pilots but we need a short checklist and an internal approval. Quick answers inline and next steps below.

1) Evidence we need (we'll provide a checklist PDF):

   - Up to $50k equivalent total spend for a 90-day pilot, or up to $15k for a 30-day quick trial.
   - Net30 invoice terms once the invoice is issued.

3) Approvals and documentation:

- Elena — please send vendor billing details and attach your VAT or tax cert. If you can provide SOC 2 or a link, that will speed approval.

From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: MAP draft: tech validation checklist + model slate (post-demo)

Alex — really helpful notes and thanks for the attachments. I parsed the PDF and the XLS and left a few inline comments in the MAP. Quick summary / next steps from Redwood: 

1) Test harness + inputs
- We will reuse your sample traces from the XLS (thank you) and augment with a synthetic high-seq tail (2.5% of requests > 1k tokens) to stress KV cache.
- Deliverable: test-harness repo + runbook (will share by Tue 07/21).

2) Success criteria (proposal)
- 95th percentile latency <= 350ms for 256-token requests (hosted).
- 95th percentile latency <= 140ms for 256-token requests (Dedicated GPU pool, p3/4-equivalent).
- Per-inference token cost: hosted target $0.00011/token; dedicated operational cost target $0.000035/token (post-quant).
- Model quality: match current production prompts with <3% regression in key intent accuracy per your eval set.

3) Model shortlist
- Redwood-bert-7B-quant (fastest token throughput)
- LlamaX-13B-fp16 (baseline quality candidate)
- OpenLM-30B-q4 (quality-first candidate)
- Redwood-micro-3B (cheap, for fallback during burst)

4) Dependencies & owners
- Security questionnaire: Priya (CareBridge) to re-share SOC2 scoping doc; Redwood to provide sample logs by 07/22.
- Data access for eval: CareBridge engineering to grant scoped S3 read access (short-term).

Can Marissa (cc'd) confirm the test harness plan and whether the latency/cost bands above are realistic from an infra POV? Ben — I added you to loop on potential Dedicated sizing and early commercial estimates.

If that aligns, we'll update the MAP PDF and track milestones in the HubSpot deal. Proposed target close for the technical milestone: Aug 20 (evaluation complete + security sign-off).

Best,
Logan Wright
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP draft: tech validation checklist + model slate (post-demo)

Adding technical context and a few clarifications from the runtime team: 

- The 95th percentile targets Logan proposed are achievable for the curated shortlist given tuned batching and prefix cache, assuming peak concurrency <= 120 RPS sustained. If you expect spikes >200 RPS we should plan a burst buffer in Dedicated sizing.

- Cost math notes: the hosted token rate is an estimate based on current multi-tenant pricing and assumes standard batching; quantized dedicated runs (Q4_0) reach the $0.000035/token operational band under steady-state with 8-card nodes. We'll provide a cost projection table (tokens/sec vs cost) by Tue.

- Model selection guidance for the eval:
  * Start with LlamaX-13B-fp16 as the quality baseline (good balance of latency + semantic fidelity).
  * Run Redwood-bert-7B-quant as the speed-first candidate for the same prompt set.
  * Include OpenLM-30B-q4 on a smaller subset for quality checks only (costly but useful signal).

- Benchmarks we'll record: p50/p95/p99 latency, median tokens/sec, DBR (drop-in rate), and the eval accuracy delta vs your golden set.

Deliverables I will upload: updated MAP PDF with explicit milestone owner table, a runbook link for the harness, and a cost projection CSV. Expect those by end of day 07/21.

Marissa
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

Adding technical context and a few clarifications from the runtime team: 

- The 95th percentile targets Logan proposed are achievable for the curated shortlist given tuned batching and prefix cache, assuming peak concurrency <= 120 RPS sustained. If you expect spikes >200 RPS we should plan a burst buffer in Dedicated sizing.

- Cost math notes: the hosted token rate is an estimate based on current multi-tenant pricing and assumes standard batching; quantized dedicated runs (Q4_0) reach the $0.000035/token operational band under steady-state with 8-card nodes. We'll provide a cost projection table (tokens/sec vs cost) by Tue.

- Model selection guidance for the eval:
  * Start with LlamaX-13B-fp16 as the quality baseline (good balance of latency + semantic fidelity).
  * Run Redwood-bert-7B-quant as the speed-first candidate for the same prompt set.
  * Include OpenLM-30B-q4 on a smaller subset for quality checks only (costly but useful signal).

- Benchmarks we'll record: p50/p95/p99 latency, median tokens/sec, DBR (drop-in rate), and the eval accuracy delta vs your golden set.

Deliverables I will upload: updated MAP PDF with explicit milestone owner table, a runbook link for the harness, and a cost projection CSV. Expect those by end of day 07/21.
